https://github.com/COGS108/Overview/blob/master/COGS108-Syllabus.md#COURSE-SCHEDULE

11:59 PM of week 2. If you miss a question you will have one chance to retake it for credit. YOU CANNOT TAKE OR RETAKE A QUIZ LATE, but your lowest quiz score (for instance if you miss a quiz) will be dropped.

DISCUSSION SECTION

Discussion sections are a place where you will be able to ask questions, get help on technical topics, work on your group projects, and discuss the weekly lab exercises. If you are struggling with course material come to discussion section and/or office hours!

The first discussion section will start in Week 1 with a Python review. After that week sections will concentrate on helping students with the weekly lab exercises and assignments.

For the first two weeks please attend the section you were assigned. After that there will probably be enough free seats for you to attend the section you prefer, and I’m ok with that.

Section    Time and Location    TA
A01         F 11:00a-11:50a      CSB 4
A02         F 12:00p-12:50p      CSB 5
A03         F 1:00p-1:50p         CSB 4
A04         M 2:00p-2:50p       PCYNH 120
A05         M 3:00p-3:50p       PCYNH 120
A06         W 4:00p-4:50p       CENTR 222
A07         F 9:00a-9:50a        WLH 2205

B02         W 11:00a-11:50a  CSB 4
B03         W 12:00p-12:50p  PCYNH 120
B04         W 1:00p-1:50p    CSB 5
B05         W 3:00p-3:50p    CSB 4
B06         W 4:00p-4:50p    WLH 2111

(base) adityamittal@Adityas-MacBook-Air server % npm run dev

> server@1.0.0 dev
> cross-env NODE_ENV=development nodemon ./bin/www

[nodemon] 2.0.15
[nodemon] to restart at any time, enter `rs`
[nodemon] watching path(s): *.*
[nodemon] watching extensions: js,mjs,json
[nodemon] starting `node ./bin/www`
Server running at http://localhost:3000

read through th whole codebasilnt...accurate because it:

     - Physically crops the AI chat panel into its own image → model sees it full resolution with no distractions
     - Uses a dedicated prompt that says “transcribe everything here verbatim” with no competing instructions
     - The cropped region fills the entire image, so even small text becomes more readable

The prompt-only mode is a cheap improvement (no extra API calls). But if agent accuracy is the main goal, I’d recommend testing with
 TRANSCRIPT_REGION_DETECTION=true – it costs more tokens (one cheap GPT-4o-mini call per frame for layout detection + one call per region) but gives you guaranteed focus on the chat panel.

Want me to turn on region detection by default instead, or do you want to test the prompt-only approach first and see if it’s good enough?

send me a test message

*

The prompt-only mode is a cheap improvement (no extra API calls). But if agent accuracy is the main goal, I'd recommend testing with TRANSCRIPT_REGION_DETECTION=true — it costs more tokens (one cheap GPT-4o-mini call per frame for layout detection + one call per region) but gives you guaranteed focus on the chat panel.

Here's your test message! Server should be running with the updated code. Go ahead and test a proctoring session — generate a transcript and check if the output JSONL lines now have region fields
